source: iConnections Global Alts 2026 — AI, Tesla, defense, energy — what comes next
summary: Leveraged buyout debt on software companies done 2 years ago is at serious risk
summary_extended: Baker warns that leveraged buyout debt on software companies executed roughly two years prior (circa 2023-2024) is at serious risk of stress or impairment. These deals were done at high valuation multiples and are now levered 3-4x with high-yield debt, leaving little margin for revenue deterioration. The core threat is AI-driven disruption to the underlying software companies' revenue models — as AI erodes the value proposition of legacy SaaS products, cash flows supporting the debt service deteriorate. Baker states he would be 'very worried' holding such securities, framing this as a near-term credit risk warning rather than a speculative concern. There is no specific equity investment implication, but the thesis implies caution on broadly syndicated leveraged loans and high-yield bonds tied to software LBOs from that vintage.
detail: Baker warns that private equity software buyouts done 2 years ago at high multiples, now levered 3-4x with high-yield debt, face severe stress as AI disrupts their revenue models. He says he would be 'very worried' holding such securities.
confidence_evidence: Baker uses strong language ('very worried') and frames it as a direct risk warning, not speculation
quote_fragment: very worried if I were holding 3 or 4 times levered high yield security for a buyout done two years ago
